title: Israel Claims Assassination of Iran's Security Chief Larijani and Basij Commander
summary: In a major escalation on Day 18 of the conflict, Israel claims to have killed Ali Larijani and Gholamreza Soleimani. Tehran has not confirmed, and a handwritten note from Larijani has raised questions about proof of life.

title: Trump's confidence is undimmed — but every Iran option comes with risk
summary: The president is acting like business as usual, but rising gas prices and reluctant allies are making the Iran conflict a political headache that won't go away.

title: UN Security Council Convenes Emergency Session Over Gaza Ceasefire Collapse
summary: World leaders demand accountability as fragile truce breaks down along the Gaza corridor, leaving humanitarian aid convoys stranded and millions at risk.

title: Prime Minister to Launch 'Farmer Card' Initiative in Tangail on Pohela Boishakh
summary: Prime Minister Tarique Rahman is scheduled to inaugurate the government's landmark 'Farmer Card' program on the upcoming Pohela Boishakh, marking a significant step in digitizing agricultural subsidies.

title: Fizz Cleared for Play Following Knee Injury in Series Win Over Pakistan
summary: The Bangladesh Cricket Board has confirmed that pace spearhead Mustafizur Rahman has been cleared of any serious injury after a mid-pitch scare during Sunday's series-deciding ODI against Pakistan.

title: G20 Summit in Johannesburg Closes With Landmark Climate Finance Pact
summary: Developed nations pledge $500 billion over a decade to support green transitions in emerging economies, though critics call the timeline dangerously slow.

title: Dhaka Rapid Transit Line 6 Reaches 500,000 Daily Rider Milestone
summary: Bangladesh's flagship urban rail project exceeds ridership projections six months ahead of schedule, prompting calls to accelerate Line 2 construction.

title: FC Barcelona Signs Teenage Prodigy in Record-Breaking 180M Euro Deal
summary: The Catalan giants break the world transfer record as they secure the Argentine wunderkind amid fierce competition from Premier League clubs.

title: India-Pakistan Diplomatic Channel Reopens After Three-Year Freeze
summary: Back-channel talks facilitated by Oman have yielded a provisional agreement to resume consular services and ease trade restrictions along the Wagah border.

title: Anti-Corruption Commission Files Charges Against 14 LGRD Officials
summary: The sweeping crackdown targets a procurement scandal involving ghost infrastructure projects worth nearly Tk 800 crore across four southern districts.

title: Shakib Al Hasan Named Bangladesh ODI Captain for World Cup Campaign
summary: The board's decision ends weeks of speculation, with the veteran all-rounder set to lead a squad featuring several promising newcomers.
